metadata.source_note: Official chaptered law. Sections 2, 5, and 7 establish tax-year-2026 personal-income-tax modifications for qualified passenger vehicle loan interest, qualified small business stock gain, and federal bonus depreciation; section 3 raises the refundable Oregon earned income credit percentages to 14 and 17 percent; and sections 12 through 14 create and limit a nonrefundable new-jobs credit. The official publication notes that sections 2 and 7 were identified as subjects of an April 2026 referendum petition and preserves the petition-status endnote as part of the authoritative source.
metadata.title: Oregon Laws 2026, chapter 142 (SB 1507): Revenue

metadata.source_note: Official chaptered law. metadata.title: Oregon Laws 2026, chapter 142 (SB 1507): Revenue

metadata.source_note: Official chaptered law corresponding to enrolled HB 4084-A. Section 16 amends section 12, chapter 142, Oregon Laws 2026 (SB 1507), to restrict the new-jobs credit to taxpayers engaged primarily in a qualified industry and to jobs created in a qualified industry, defines the qualifying industry categories, and adds certification and rulemaking requirements. Section 17 gives the Act a June 5, 2026 effective date.
metadata.title: Oregon Laws 2026, chapter 50 (HB 4084): Economic development

metadata.source_note: Official chaptered law corresponding to enrolled SB 1510-A. Sections 9 through 12 extend the elective pass-through business alternative income tax and member credit through tax years beginning before January 1, 2028, and allow a pass-through entity to apply an overpayment to the subsequent tax year's estimated tax.
metadata.title: Oregon Laws 2026, chapter 75 (SB 1510): Taxation
